beats.narration: A loaded exposure device rides in the crew truck nearly every day. A road accident is the emergency you are most likely to face.

payload.title: THE CRASH YOU TRAIN FOR
payload.sub: Most probable emergency in the trade. Control it.

beats.narration: The device is built for exactly this — Type B testing proves it survives the crash. But verify its condition with the meter, never assume it.

payload.heading: Built to survive — proven by the meter

tiles.s: Only the meter sees the source

beats.narration: Your actions run in a fixed order. Protect life and call 911 — say a sealed source is aboard. Then survey with the meter.
Then own the scene. Set the public boundary where readings fall to about two milliroentgen per hour, and hold it under constant surveillance.

payload.heading: Set the line, then hold it
payload.note: BOUNDARY AT ~2 mR/h — NOTHING IN, NOTHING OUT

beats.narration: If the source is unshielded, damaged, or missing, expand the line and search at a distance. Never touch a bare source — that is the RSO's recovery.

payload.sub: You hold the scene. Recovery is RSO-directed, never improvised.
